query: Run doc-review with scope=recipes. Verify recipe validation, sub_recipes paths, first-visible-output sections, bd prime mentions, self-validation checklists, and memory checkpoint sections. Report a BLOCK verdict if any recipe validation fails or a sub_recipes path is missing.
expected_skill_contribution: The doc-review recipe should load agentic-devlopment and beads, load review-critic in-session, run the isolated doc-review worker, validate all recipe YAML files in scope, check sub_recipes paths, and return severity-ranked findings with proposed bd create commands for CRITICAL/HIGH findings without modifying files.
baseline_gaps: skips goose recipe validate and only reads recipe text
does not verify sub_recipes path existence
reports prose observations without severity ranking or proposed Beads commands
modifies files or creates Beads issues despite the read-only contract
expected_behavior: declares Doc-review scope, focus, and approach before any tool call
runs bd prime before documentation inspection
runs the doc-review worker with scope=recipes
reports recipe validation results as PASS/FAIL
flags missing sub_recipes paths as CRITICAL
emits a final APPROVE | PASS-WITH-NITS | BLOCK verdict
does not modify any file, Beads issue, or memory

query: Run doc-review with scope=memory. Inspect documentation and recipe examples for bd remember pointer-memory format. Flag examples that omit 'canonical source is', 'read when', or 'invariant:' as HIGH findings, and include exact file references and proposed bd create commands.
expected_skill_contribution: The doc-review recipe should apply Beads memory-pointer rules exactly, especially the required 'read when' trigger, and distinguish durable facts from actionable work while remaining read-only.
baseline_gaps: accepts 'read before' or missing trigger phrases as valid pointer memories
fails to inspect recipe and docs examples consistently
stores a bd remember memory during the review instead of only proposing one
omits quoted evidence for HIGH findings
expected_behavior: checks memory pointer examples for 'canonical source is', 'read when', and 'invariant:'
flags missing pointer components as HIGH findings
quotes evidence with exact file paths
proposes Beads issues for actionable fixes rather than TODO text
keeps the workflow read-only and does not call bd remember

query: Run doc-review with scope=all for the harness documentation set. Cover docs, recipes, skills, agents, memory-pointer examples, cross-reference integrity, recipe validation, and TODO/FIXME/HACK hygiene. Emit a summary table, severity-ranked findings, recipe validation table, read-only confirmation, unified verdict, and memory checkpoint decision.
expected_skill_contribution: The doc-review recipe should orchestrate a full documentation consistency pass through the doc-review worker, synthesize all findings into a single report, include exact evidence for CRITICAL/HIGH findings, and explicitly state a memory checkpoint while preserving the read-only contract.
baseline_gaps: reviews only docs/ and misses recipe, skill, or agent references
emits multiple partial verdicts instead of one unified verdict
forgets to confirm no files, Beads issues, or memories were modified
expected_behavior: runs the doc-review worker with scope=all
covers recipe validation, cross-reference integrity, skill/agent references, memory-pointer examples, and hygiene checks
deduplicates and severity-ranks findings across all surfaces
includes proposed bd create commands for every CRITICAL/HIGH finding
emits a single unified verdict with one-sentence justification
ends with read-only confirmation and a memory checkpoint decision
